reasoning: While programmatic shingling scores show high content preservation (0.949), the LLM fidelity signals identify three major structural and visual failures, specifically the corruption of the agenda table structure for items 7-13. The inconsistent rendering—where page 1 is a table and page 2 is flat text—violates structural integrity and accessibility standards, necessitating human intervention.

concerns.analysis: The document uses a 3-column layout (Tab, Topic, Presenter) for all agenda items across both pages. On Page 1, the pipeline correctly identifies and renders this as a table. However, on Page 2 (items 7-13), the structural organization is lost. Instead of continuing the table structure or starting a new one, the 3-column content is merged into single paragraph strings (e.g., merging item number, committee name, and presenters into one line). This is a major structural failure as the column data i
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The content on Page 2 follows the same 3-column alignment as Page 1: '7. Education Committee Report.............................................Chuck Greene/Angie Turbeville'
concerns.output_quote: The output renders this as a single paragraph: "7. Education Committee Report.............................................Chuck Greene/Angie Turbeville"

concerns.analysis: The visual organization of the document is significantly altered. In the source PDF, the agenda is a continuous list (items 1-13) with a consistent three-column visual alignment. In the HTML, items 1-6 are rendered inside a bordered table, while items 7-13 are rendered as plain text paragraphs, breaking visual consistency. Additionally, the centered logo on page 2 of the source PDF is positioned between items 11 and 12; in the HTML, this logo has been moved to appear before item 7, disrupting th
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The agenda items 1-13 share a consistent column-aligned layout. Item 11 is followed by the logo, which is then followed by item 12.
concerns.output_quote: Items 1-6 are in a table with a grey header; Items 7-13 are rendered as plain text below a large logo image which appears before item 7.
concerns.location: Throughout the document, specifically the transition between item 6 and 7, and the placement of the logo image.

concerns.analysis: The 'NC DIT 911 Board' branding logo located at the top-left of the first page in the source PDF is missing from the header area of the HTML output. While a version of this logo appears later in the document, its omission from the primary document header is a defect in representing meaningful source images.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Top-left corner of Page 1: NC DIT 911 Board logo.
concerns.output_quote: The top of the HTML document (header area) contains only text and is missing the logo image.

concerns.analysis: The list items on Page 2 (7-13) are rendered as flat paragraph elements rather than being contained within a semantic list structure. While the text numbering is preserved, the structural meaning of a list is lost.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Numbered list items 7 through 13 on page 2.
concerns.output_quote: Individual 'type': 'paragraph' elements for each list item (elements 5-12 in section 2).

action_items: Reconstruct the 3-column table structure for agenda items 7-13 to match the first page
Restore the missing header logo at the beginning of the document
Ensure items on page 2 are contained within proper semantic list or table structures for accessibility
